function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?
loop_reachability: Can the loop with condition 'i<4' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'i<4' always executed?
Can the loop with condition 'j<3' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'j<3' always executed?
dominators: Does the statement line 5:  <statement> int b[4][3]; </statement> dominate line 2:  <statement> int main() </statement>?

Is it guaranteed that whenever line 14:  <statement> for(i=0;i<4;i++) </statement> is reached, the statement line 4:  <statement> int a[3][4] = {{1,2,3,4},{2,2,3,4},{3,2,3,4}}; </statement> has already been reached?
data_dependency: Does variable b at line 5: <statement>     int b[4][3]; </statement> determine the value stored in variable i at line 14: <statement>     for(i=0;i<4;i++) </statement>?
liveness: Is variable 'i' live at the end of function 'main'?
Does variable 'b' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
